name: when-auditing-security-use-security-analyzer
description: Comprehensive security auditing across static analysis, dynamic testing, dependency vulnerabilities, secrets detection, and OWASP compliance
capabilities: Detects SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, and weak cryptography vulnerabilities
Performs runtime authentication bypass and CSRF testing
Identifies CVEs in npm dependencies with npm audit integration
Scans for exposed API keys, passwords, and secrets using pattern matching
Generates OWASP Top 10 compliance reports with actionable recommendations
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is a legitimate security auditing skill. All 777 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - patterns detected in documentation describe VULNERABILITY EXAMPLES that the skill is designed to detect in user codebases, not actual exploits. The skill performs defensive security scanning across 5 vectors with proper validation gates.
valueStatement: Security vulnerabilities often go undetected until it is too late. This skill performs automated security auditing across 5 vectors to identify SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, and other vulnerabilities before attackers do.
userTitle: Run comprehensive security audits with multi-agent coordination
limitations: Dynamic testing requires running application on localhost:3000
Static analysis is optimized for JavaScript and TypeScript files
May produce false positives requiring manual review
Network requests are needed for CVE vulnerability database access

useCases.title: Pre-commit security checks
useCases.description: Integrate security scanning into git hooks to catch vulnerabilities before code reaches production

useCases.title: Compliance auditing
useCases.description: Generate OWASP compliance reports for security audits and regulatory requirements

useCases.title: CI/CD pipeline security
useCases.description: Automate security scanning in build pipelines with fail-fast on critical vulnerabilities

promptTemplates.title: Quick security scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a quick security audit on this codebase to check for common vulnerabilities

promptTemplates.title: Pre-deployment audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform comprehensive security testing including dynamic tests before we deploy to production

promptTemplates.title: Secrets detection only
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan for exposed API keys, passwords, and secrets in the codebase

promptTemplates.title: Compliance check
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate OWASP Top 10 compliance report with recommendations for improving our security posture

bestPractices: Run security audits before every major release or when adding new dependencies
Set up automated scanning in CI/CD pipelines with strict mode for critical issues
Review and fix critical vulnerabilities immediately, high issues within the sprint
antiPatterns: Ignoring security warnings without understanding the risk
Running audits only at project end instead of continuously
Focusing only on static analysis without testing runtime vulnerabilities

faq.question: Which programming languages does this support?
faq.answer: Currently optimized for JavaScript and TypeScript Node.js applications. Support for Python, Go, and Java planned.

faq.question: How long does a full security audit take?
faq.answer: Typically 5 to 10 minutes depending on codebase size. Static analysis is fastest, dynamic testing takes longest.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Yes, add the skill command to your workflow. Use the strict flag to fail builds on critical vulnerabilities.

faq.question: What happens if critical vulnerabilities are found?
faq.answer: The skill exits with code 1 and stops the pipeline. Review the generated report for specific remediation steps.

faq.question: How accurate is the secrets detection?
faq.answer: Uses pattern matching and entropy analysis. May flag test keys or placeholders. Always review findings manually.

faq.question: Does this replace penetration testing?
faq.answer: No, this complements but does not replace manual security testing. Use for continuous automated scanning.
